fr.elements: Main Building; Roof System; Landscaped Area; Access Road
fr.considerations: Dense Vegetation; Limited Side Access; Single Access Point
fr.recommendations: Improving responder access and operational efficiency at the property could be supported by addressing the dense vegetation that restricts movement and visibility. Trimming or clearing plant growth around the access road may enhance the accessibility and maneuverability of emergency vehicles. Ensuring that clear pathways are maintained may assist emergency personnel in navigating quickly and safely. Additionally, enhancing side access where possible could facilitate more efficient staging and movement during emergency operations, reducing response time. Effectively marking the single access point with visible signage may assist responders in quickly identifying and reaching the entry under pressing conditions.

wildfire.conditions: Scattered Trees; Scattered Shrubs; Vegetated Perimeter; Open Terrain; Vegetated Access Route; Limited Defensible Space; Vegetation Adjacent to Structure; Vegetation Adjacent to Access Route; Open Terrain; Scattered Shrubs
wildfire.recommendations: Enhancing defensible space by increasing the separation between the scattered trees and structures, as well as managing the scattered shrubs in the immediate vicinity of the property, could improve wildfire resilience. Prioritizing the management of vegetation adjacent to the structures and access routes may reduce potential fire exposure and support both evacuation routes and firefighter access. Maintaining open terrain around the property can further reduce fuel continuity, providing a buffer that minimizes potential fire spread. It would be beneficial to focus on maintaining a clear vegetated perimeter by selectively reducing combustible vegetation concentrations, thereby preserving open areas that can act as fuel breaks. Long-term vegetation management strategies, such as regular trimming and removal of dense underbrush, will continue to support the property's preparedness and resilience against wildfire threats.
